response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and web developer with over 15 years of industry experience, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The single most disqualifying factor is the absolute requirement for native-level Japanese language proficiency, which the candidate does not possess — his listed languages are English, Romanian, and German. Beyond that hard blocker, his technical background is almost entirely web, TypeScript/Node.js, and application-layer development; he has no demonstrated experience in network security (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal), L2/L3 networking, switch architectures, or Linux kernel-level development with source modification. While he has touched C/C++ in limited contexts and worked briefly with embedded systems at National Instruments and Altom, these are peripheral to his career and fall well short of the depth required for embedded firmware security work on LAN switches.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience, demonstrating adaptability and sustained technical competence across multiple domains and languages
Has hands-on C and C++ exposure through National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI) and a bachelor thesis project using Linux, Jack audio, and C++, showing some low-level systems familiarity
Embedded systems exposure at Altom Consulting (robotic arms, DLang/Java hardware control) indicates some willingness and ability to work outside the web stack
concerns: Does not meet the mandatory native-level Japanese language requirement — an absolute hard blocker for this role
No experience with network security technologies, LAN switching, L2/L3 protocols, MACsec, 802.1X, SNMP, NETCONF, or any of the core technical domains listed in the job description
Career is overwhelmingly web and application-layer focused (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, GraphQL); lacks the embedded firmware and Linux kernel modification experience required
